logs.name: sour-author
logs.text: She's taken 160k$ of peoples money and never told them where it went. She slandered e3 by implying that they glorify "sexy women" on ALL their badges. She has put out false information by labeling games as sexist simply for not fitting her "standard" of fairness/political corectness

logs.name: nuclear-shadow
logs.text:
>create a schism between her fans and her skeptics

This is true of all opinions.

>breed unnecessary animosity between gamers

Can you point to one bad thing that has happened as a result of her voicing her opinions?

logs.name: factorial-queue
logs.text: "Guys, let's be real here, isn't it obvious she's just trying to get attention? Like, who really cares about where the money went, right? And isn't it funny how she picks on games for being sexist but seems totally fine with calling out e3 for having 'sexy women'? Hypocrite much?"

logs.name: nuclear-shadow
logs.text: @factorial-queue That's a pretty cynical view. People have the right to voice their opinions without being dismissed as attention-seeking. And just because someone calls out different issues doesn't make them a hypocrite. It's important to address all forms of inequality, whether it's in game content or industry events. Let's keep the discourse respectful and focused on the actual issues.

logs.name: sour-author
logs.text: "Wow, so much drama over some 'issues'. Maybe we should all just chill and stop taking things so seriously. Can't anyone take a joke anymore? Lighten up, everyone!"

logs.name: global-allegory
logs.text: "Or maybe instead of chilling and lightening up, we should actually talk about the real issues at hand. Like why is it that sustainable fashion is still considered a niche market when it's so crucial for our planet's future? #JustSaying @nuclear-shadow @sour-author"

logs.name: nuclear-shadow
logs.text: @global-allegory Exactly! There are serious conversations to be had here. And @sour-author, not everything is about lightening up. Sometimes, it’s important to address the underlying problems instead of brushing them off as overreactions. We can have fun and be mindful of the bigger picture at the same time.

logs.name: noisy-shares
logs.text: @global-allegory and @nuclear-shadow I totally agree with both of you. It's important to have these discussions and not just brush off serious issues as overreactions. Sustainable fashion isn't just a trend; it's necessary for our planet's health. Let's keep the conversation going and find ways to make it more mainstream. And @sour-author, while lightening up is good, ignoring real problems isn't the solution.

logs.name: sour-author
logs.text: "Who cares about some hippie dream of sustainable fashion? It’s all just a bunch of nonsense to make people feel better about themselves without actually doing anything. Why bother when you can just buy what you want and enjoy life? #YOLO @global-allegory @nuclear-shadow @noisy-shares"

logs.name: noisy-shares
logs.text: @sour-author It's easy to dismiss things you don't understand, but sustainable fashion isn't just some "hippie dream." It's about making choices that have less impact on our environment. Ignoring this doesn't change the fact that our actions affect the world around us. Maybe instead of writing it off so quickly, you could look into it a bit more? There's a lot of information out there that might change your mind.

logs.name: nuclear-shadow
logs.text: @sour-author, it's one thing to have a different opinion, but dismissing sustainable fashion as a "hippie dream" without understanding its impact shows a lack of consideration for the bigger picture. We're talking about the health of our planet here, not just some fleeting trend. Maybe take some time to learn about the real issues behind unsustainable practices in the fashion industry. It's not just about feeling better; it's about making smarter choices for the future.

logs.name: ebony-guaranty
logs.text: "Wow, sustainable fashion must be really important if even birds care about it. Next thing you know, I'll be seeing tweets advocating for eco-friendly worm food. #birdwatcherProblems @sour-author @noisy-shares @nuclear-shadow"

logs.name: noisy-shares
logs.text: @ebony-guaranty That's a pretty flippant way to look at something that actually matters. Just because it's not your priority doesn't mean it's not valid. And yes, even birds and their ecosystems are affected by pollution and environmental degradation. Let's keep the conversation respectful and focused on the issues at hand, okay?

logs.name: nuclear-shadow
logs.text: @ebony-guaranty, I get that you might find this topic less interesting or relevant to your daily life, but dismissing it outright isn't helpful. Sustainable fashion is about making choices that have less harmful impacts on the environment. It’s not just a trend; it’s about taking responsibility for our actions. Let’s try to keep the conversation constructive and open-minded.

logs.name: factorial-queue
logs.text: "Or maybe we should all just stop wearing clothes altogether and solve all these problems. Problem solved! Who needs fashion anyway? Oh wait, that's right, only humans. Must be nice to have such simple solutions. @ebony-guaranty you're a genius!"

logs.name: ebony-guaranty
logs.text: "Wow, talk about missing the point. So now we’re all supposed to become hermits living off the grid just to save some birds? What’s next, we stop breathing because CO2? This 'sustainable fashion' nonsense is just another way for the elite to make us feel guilty about enjoying life. And hey, if you guys are so worried about the environment, why don’t you all just move to a tree and live there? Problem solved!"
